2603.28575.title: ChemCLIP: Bridging Organic and Inorganic Anticancer Compounds Through Contrastive Learning
2603.28575.paper_summary: The discovery of anticancer therapeutics has traditionally treated organic small molecules and metal-based coordination complexes as separate chemical domains, limiting knowledge transfer despite their shared biological objectives. This disparity is particularly pronounced in available data, with extensive screening databases for organic compounds compared to only a few thousand characterized metal complexes. Here, we introduce ChemCLIP, a dual-encoder contrastive learning framework that bridges this organic-inorganic divide by learning unified representations based on shared anticancer activities rather than structural similarity. We compiled complementary datasets comprising 44,854 unique organic compounds and 5,164 unique metal complexes, standardized across 60 cancer cell lines. By training parallel encoders with activity-aware hard negative mining, we mapped structurally distinct compounds into a shared 256-dimensional embedding space where biologically similar compounds cluster together regardless of chemical class. We systematically evaluated four molecular encoding strategies: Morgan fingerprints, ChemBERTa, MolFormer, and Chemprop, through quantitative alignment metrics, embedding visualizations, and downstream classification tasks. Morgan fingerprints achieved superior performance with an average alignment ratio of 0.899 and downstream classification AUCs of 0.859 (inorganic) and 0.817 (organic). This work establishes contrastive learning as an effective strategy for unifying disparate chemical domains and provides empirical guidance for encoder selection in multi-modal chemistry applications, with implications extending beyond anticancer drug discovery to any scenario requiring cross-domain chemical knowledge transfer.
2603.28575.paper_summary_zh: 抗癌療法的發現傳統上將有機小分子和金屬基配位化合物視為不同的化學領域，儘管它們的生物學目標相同，但這限制了知識的轉移。這種差異在可用數據中尤為明顯，因為有機化合物的篩選數據庫非常廣泛，而被表徵的金屬配合物僅有幾千個。在這裡，我們介紹了ChemCLIP，一個雙編碼器對比學習框架，通過基於共享抗癌活性而非結構相似性學習統一表示，來彌合這一有機-無機的鴻溝。我們編輯了互補數據集，包括44,854個獨特的有機化合物和5,164個獨特的金屬配合物，這些數據集在60個癌細胞系中標準化。通過使用活動感知的硬負樣本挖掘訓練平行編碼器，我們將結構上不同的化合物映射到一個共享的256維嵌入空間中，在這裡生物學上相似的化合物無論化學類別如何都聚集在一起。我們系統地評估了四種分子編碼策略：Morgan指紋、ChemBERTa、MolFormer和Chemprop，通過定量對齊指標、嵌入可視化和下游分類任務。Morgan指紋以平均對齊比率0.899和下游分類AUC 0.859（無機）及0.817（有機）的優越表現取得了最佳效果。本研究確立了對比學習作為統一不同化學領域的有效策略，並為多模態化學應用中的編碼器選擇提供了實證指導，其影響超越抗癌藥物發現，適用於任何需要跨領域化學知識轉移的情境。
2603.28575.author: Mohamad Koohi-Moghadam et.al.
2603.28575.authors: Mohamad Koohi-Moghadam, Hongzhe Sun, Hongyan Li, Kyongtae Tyler Bae
